column3: English
Praise of the Lord
The Lord without beginning before the beginning, and infinite beyond the end.
First, let His glory beautify this book.

column1: বিনা লক্ষে শূন্য পরে স্থাপিছে আকাশ।
column3: Without aim or target He set the sky upon the void.
He has made manifest the sun, the moon, and the stars.
The doer of all things Himself maintains their beauty and order.
In every manner He keeps the course of deeds under rule.
From this very day He grants life to all that moves and does not move.
By His power the span of life goes on without cessation.

column1: দৃষ্টার দৃশ্যার পরে তার দিব্য জ্যোতি।
column3: Upon the seen and the seer His divine light rests.

column1: শ্রোতার শ্রবণ মাঝে সেই দিছে শ্রুতি॥
column3: Within the hearing of the hearer He gives the faculty of hearing.
His glory pervades all beings and is beyond all counting.

column1: অতুল মহিমা তার দিতে নারি সীমা॥
column3: His incomparable glory — I am unable to give it any limit.
In the three worlds there is no other second creator.

column1: ষট রেখা পাল্য মাল্য স্বামী সর্বজন॥
column3: Lord of all people, wearing the garland of the six directions.
From Him have issued forth the bodies of all.
And again, back to Him all follow.
To contemplate His nature, the wise are bound helpless.

column1: বুঝিতে মরম তান হয় অন্ধ ধন্দ।
column3: To understand His innermost secret, one falls into blind bewilderment.
Whatever life there is, it is His creation.

column1: সংসার অসার জান সেই মাত্র সার॥
column3: Know the world to be without essence; He alone is the essence.

column1: জ্ঞান এক কণা হেন্তে পথ দর্শায়।
column3: From a single particle of His knowledge He shows the path.

column1: কৃপা রূঢ় হেন্তে কর্ম সুশম করায়॥
column3: By the force of His grace He makes all deeds beautiful and proper.

column1: তারে দণ্ডবৎ না করিল যেই শিরে।
column3: Whoever did not bow the head in prostration before Him —
the door of liberation is locked for that one.

column1: শ্রোত জ্যোতি রাখি তম শিশির নিকট।
column3: He keeps flowing light near darkness and dew.

column1: এক না পাসরি ভেক্ষ দেয় প্রতি ঘট॥
column3: Forgetting not one, He bestows His gift upon every vessel.
He brings the Ganges of compassion and scatters it without aim.

column1: ভিক্ষুকেরে দেয় নিধি কে তারে বুঝায়॥
column3: He gives treasure to the beggar — who can make Him understand?
The learned, reading and reckoning, contemplate Him.

column1: যে তাঁকে জানিল দৃড় সকল পাসরে॥
column3: Whoever has come to know Him firmly forgets everything else.

column1: অগ্নিময়ী শিলা দাহ তন্ত্র জন্ত্র রিতে।
column3: By the method of tantra and mantra the fiery stone burns.
The happiness of all rests within the one Lord.

column1: যে সেবে ঈশ্বর শ্মরে আছে একজন।
column3: Whoever serves and remembers the Lord, remaining as one individual —

column1: সে করে সকল কর্ম আলগ আপন॥
column3: that one performs all deeds freely and independently.
From Him alone all living beings receive their rightful portion.

column1: অনন্ত অপার লীলা বুঝিতে না পারি॥
column3: His infinite and boundless play — I am unable to comprehend it.

column1: সেই সে করিছে সুর শশির প্রকট।
column3: He it is who makes the gods and the moon manifest.
White light and dark light — both are plainly revealed within.
His book — may one hear it continually without rest.

column1: তাঁহার আদেশ বিনা কিছু না বুঝেন্তু॥
column3: Without His command, let nothing be understood.
He makes radiant the midst of the heart.
Through His bestowal all receive strength and power.
That jewel — fitting to it is the greatness of all.

column1: না জানে তাহার কর্তা আছে কোন ঠাই॥
column3: One does not know where that maker abides.
In the form of life He holds sovereign right over every vessel.

column1: কোন স্থানে থাকে সেই চিনিতে না পারি॥
column3: In what place He dwells — that I cannot recognize.

column1: অঙ্গবাসী নিজ রতু না পারে চিনিতে।
column3: The jewel dwelling within the body — one cannot recognize one's own jewel.

column1: যে তাকে সৃজিছে তাঁকে চিনিবে কি মতে॥
column3: how then can one know the one who created him?

column1: জীবকর্তা জীব হেতে আছে যে নিকট।
column3: The maker of life is near to the living being.
Through wisdom and through grace he becomes manifest to knowledge.
He who shows that path sees what is beyond compare.
Utterly free of grossness, he pervades everything.
Those who contemplate all the stars, pondering auspicious and inauspicious —

column1: প্রভুর শক্রতা সেই না পারে বুঝিত॥
column3: they cannot comprehend the Lord's power.

column1: যাহারে বলয় ভাল তার মন্দ হয়।
column3: What he calls good turns bad.

column1: ভিক্ষুকেরে দেয় নিধি কে তাকে বুঝায়॥
column3: He gives treasure to a beggar — who can fathom him?
Reading, counting, through wisdom one meditates on him.

column1: যে তাকে জানিল দৃড় সকল পাসরে॥
column3: Whoever has firmly known him forgets everything else.
All that he has created throughout the three worlds, in such variety —

column1: নিয়ম করিতে নারি ভাবি এক রাতি॥
column3: I cannot enumerate it even pondering a single night.

column1: বুদ্ধিবন্ত পথ যেবা পাইছে তা হেতে।
column3: The path that the intelligent one has found thereby —

column1: ধন্দ হই চাহিতে না পারে তার ভিতে॥
column3: becoming confused, one cannot reach its innermost depth.
Lost in contemplation that the many creations are one —
who can reach the end of the ever-living Creator?

column1: আদি অন্ত স্বর্গ মর্ত্য পাতালের স্থিতি।
column3: The condition of beginning and end, of heaven, earth, and the underworld —

column1: যোগ্য ভেক্ষ্য দেয় নিত্য নাহিক বিস্মৃতি॥
column3: he gives fitting sustenance daily; there is no forgetfulness in him.
Reflecting on all this in my mind, I am filled with shame.
I beg a boon: O full of grace, fulfil my desire.

column1: জ্ঞান দানে রাখ প্রভু আপন দুয়ারে।
column3: By the gift of knowledge, Lord, keep me at your own door.
Cut from me the hope of another's door.

column1: শক্তির কৃপান দিয়া কাট মন ঘোর।
column3: With the sword of power, cut away the darkness of my mind.

column1: তোর মর্ম যদি পাই সব যোগ্য মোর॥
column3: If I attain your innermost meaning, all things become fitting for me.

column1: তুমি মোর গ্রাহক কান্দিমু কার ঠাই।
column3: You are my receiver — at whose door shall I weep?

column1: তুমি মুক্তি দায়ক মাগিমু কাতে যাই॥
column3: You are the giver of liberation — where shall I go to beg?
Though in this world there are many disguises,
not a sesame-seed's worth is hidden before you.

column1: মনের মানস গুপ্ত নাই তোমা স্থানে।
column3: The secret intention of the mind is not hidden from you.

column1: তুমি সিদ্ধ কর প্রভু জানহ আপনে॥
column3: You accomplish it, Lord; you know it yourself.

column1: সেই কর্ম উত্তম তোমাতে মাগি যারে।
column3: That work which I beg of you is the best work.

column1: যে সব নবীন আমি না কহি তোমারে॥
column3: All that is new to me I do not tell you.
At your door I beg without ceasing.

column1: আর যত মনে ভাবি তুমি তার পতি॥
column3: And of all that I think in my mind, you are the master.
Powerless, of little understanding, I am a wrongdoer.

column1: তোমা ভাবি দিতেছি যে সমুদ্রে সাঁতার॥
column3: Meditating on you, I am swimming in an ocean.

column1: তুমি কৃপা করিলে তরিতে পারি সিন্ধু।
column3: If you show grace, I can cross the sea.
O Lord of the orphaned, you are the friend of the lowly.

column1: তব কৃপা বিনে কোন কার্যে নাই মুক্তি।
column3: Without your grace there is no release in any undertaking.

column1: হৃদয়ে প্রকাশি প্রভু দেও উক্তি শক্তি॥
column3: Manifest in my heart, Lord, give me the power of speech.

column1: আর কৃপা কর প্রভু দয়াল চরিত।
column3: Show more grace, Lord, O you of merciful nature —
that I may sing even a little of your glory as a hymn.
In the beginning the Lord was without example, without form.
When his conscious nature became manifest —
utterly dense with darkness, devoid of form,
the Lord's sign became filled with great radiance.
In the ocean of light, primordially, was the light of Muhammad.
Through him, the conqueror of the world received his fortune.
The first new flower of the golden garden —
the crown-jewel of wisdom and speech, illumining all the world.
From the crown of the heavens, the sovereign lord of all the Prophets —

column1: শিরয়ত জান তাঁর প্রভু পাশে গতি॥
column3: one who knows the sacred law, his movement is ever toward his Lord.
Without reading, all scriptures became known to him.
The supreme lord of the throne dwells upon the earth.

column1: সেই পুষ্প হেন্তে আদ্য আদম উজ্জল।
column3: From that flower Adam was first made radiant —

column1: সকল কদর্য পূর্ণ সেই সে নির্মল॥
column3: he who is full of all worth, he who is utterly pure.
At the last, the living beings who conquer heaven come and go —

column1: সর্ব গ্রন্থ নাশি হেলা আর গ্রন্থপতি॥
column3: all scriptures are effortlessly dissolved, and he is the lord of scriptures.

column1: তান আজ্ঞা নিরোধ হইল সার ধার।
column3: His command became the supreme and essential restraint —

column1: এক বিন্দু দোলাইতে শক্তি আছে কার॥
column3: who has the power to shake even a single drop of it?
Kings conduct their affairs according to his command —

column1: তান আজ্ঞা শীরে নৃপে বুঝে কার্য মর্ম॥
column3: kings bear his command on their heads and grasp the essence of their duty.

column1: নির্ধনী মহত্ত জানি হইয়া তুষ্ট মন।
column3: Knowing the greatness of poverty, with a contented heart —

column1: দুই জগ নৃপ হইয়া না ইচ্ছিল ধন॥
column3: though king of the two worlds, he desired no wealth.
Through his own arm's labour he earned learning and livelihood —
he begged along with guests and kinsmen.

column1: সূর্য জ্যোতি সমান ধবল অন্ধ ছায়া।
column3: White as the radiance of the sun, blind to shadow —

column1: সংসারের কে আছে আর ছায়াহীন কায়া॥
column3: who else in this world has a body free of shadow?
Without the command of kings, life reaches its limit —

column1: প্রলয় পর্যন্ত সার তান আজ্ঞা বিধি॥
column3: his command and ordinance is the essence until the dissolution of the world.

column1: অধোগতি হেল যেই উচ্চ হৈল শির।
column3: Whoever was fallen low, his head was raised high —

column1: যে পড়িল হেন্তে ধরি করিল স্থিরস্থির॥
column3: whoever had fallen, he took hold of them and made them firmly steady.

column1: যেই দুষ্ট ভ্রষ্ট তারে হৈলা সব নষ্ট।
column3: Whoever was wicked and corrupt — everything of theirs was destroyed.

column1: যে শিষ্ট উৎকৃষ্ট তারে হৈলা স্পষ্ট পুষ্ট॥
column3: Whoever was well-mannered and excellent — they were clearly nourished and thrived.

column1: যেই না মানিল সলোচনে হইয়া অন্ধ।
column3: Whoever did not accept him, becoming blind by their own eyes —

column1: অদ্যাপিও না এড়ায় সেবে বলে যন্দ॥
column3: even to this day they cannot escape; so says that witness.
The angels who dwell in heaven observe his command —

column1: তান স্তুতি কহি সেবে গোয়ারন্তু কাল॥
column3: reciting his praise, they spend their time in his service.

column1: আনে কি কহিবে যারে আপে কর্তার।
column3: What more can another say of him, when the Creator himself —
has said: for your sake I created the world?

column1: কে বুঝিতে পারে তান মহিমা প্রচণ্ড।
column3: Who can comprehend his tremendous glory?

column1: অঙ্গুলি ইঙ্গিতে যাঁর চন্দ্র দুই খণ্ড॥
column3: At whose finger's gesture the moon was cleft into two pieces.
He set a serpent upon the forest-deer as it went into the forest —

column1: শাবক না খায় দুগ্ধ আইল তৈতক্ষণে॥
column3: the fawn would not drink the milk; it came at that very moment.
An insect inside a ruby, with a blade of grass in its mouth —
he displayed it in the assembly with great wonder and delight.

column1: সেই কীটে মহিমা কহিল নানা রীতে।
column3: That insect spoke of his glory in many ways —
yet still in the hearts of sinners there is disbelief.

column1: বাক্যধারী হইয়া সর্পে যাঁর গুণ গায়।
column3: Becoming endowed with speech, the serpent sang his virtues —
on his pure body a sweet fragrance flows without ceasing.

column1: মাক্ষিক না পড়য় গায়ে পুণ্য কলেবর।
column3: No fly lands upon his sacred and meritorious body —

column1: অবিরত ঘন ছত্র যার শির পর॥
column3: above whose head a dense and ceaseless canopy of clouds hangs ever present.
Why speak of his boundless glory?
Whose virtues the Quran has proclaimed in its pure and stainless words.
His great and secret story amid the world —

column1: না কহিনু পুস্তক সমুখে আছে ভার॥
column3: I did not speak of it, for the weight of the book lies ahead.

column1: মুক্তিদাতা পাপ হস্তা তান চারি মিত।
column3: He is the liberator, the destroyer of sin — his four companions are dear.

column1: শিরয়ত গৃহ চারি শন্দ চারি ভিত॥
column3: The house of divine law has four pillars, and four are its foundations.

column1: কি কহিতে পারি আমি সে সব মহিমা।
column3: What can I say of all that glory?

column1: কেন দিতে পারে শুদ্ধ রতুআকর সীমা॥
column3: Who can give the measure of a pure treasury of jewels?
It cannot be fully told even if one were to spend all of eternity.

column1: ভাবি চিত্তি তেজিনু সে সব বাক্য জাল॥
column3: Reflecting in my mind, I abandoned that entire net of words.
Alaol's account of Nizami's path and the names of the books
(in jamak metre)
A great and noble man was Nizami Ganjavi of former times.
In the Persian tongue he lived forever.
He composed for the sovereign Shah named Alauddin.
He spoke of glory beyond compare in his books.
By his own intellect he composed many books.

column1: তার মাঝে খমছর দিতে নারি তুল॥
column3: Among them, the Khamsa has no equal.
The people of Arabia call five a khamsa.

column1: সে পথ কিতাবের নাম শুন একে॥
column3: Hear one by one the names of those books in that tradition.
That composition of devotees' wisdom, the Makhzan al-Asrar that brightens the face —
and Layla-Majnun, a tale of one passion therein.
And in the other three books, of three kings,
he told with fine taste of their glories and secrets.
The Prophet Dhul-Qarnayn, that Shah Alexander,
roamed ceaselessly over water and land throughout the world.
Waging many wars, he ruled the earth.

column1: জলে স্থলে ছন্দে বন্দে শিখাইল নীতি॥
column3: On water and land, in verse and in praise, he taught righteousness.

column1: লোকের সুশম্ব হেতু যত হৈল কাম।
column3: All the deeds done for the good repute of the people —
the name of that book is called the Iskandar-nama.

column1: আর নরপতি এক খোছেরা তার নাম।
column3: Another king — Khusrau is his name.
He is the grandson of Noshirvan, a great abode of virtues.

column1: যেন মতে শিরিনীরে দেখি ভাষ হেল।
column3: In what manner, upon seeing Shirin, he fell into longing —
with what great effort he obtained Shirin's company —
that tale of their union at length in the book is beyond compare.
The name of that book is known as Shirin-Khusrau.
The fifth book is this Sapta Paykar.
Bahram-Gor was the name of that most excellent king.

column1: তাহার রহস্য যত যে কর্ম হৈলা।
column3: All his secrets and whatever deeds came to pass —
how the pavilion named Khayamnik was built —
At the feet of that Nizami, with devotion,
I undertook to compose it in the language of payar verse.

column1: যেন মতে হেল এই কেতাব উদ্দেগ।
column3: In what manner this book came to be undertaken —

column1: প্রথমে কহিমু তাহা শুন সাধু লোক॥
column3: I shall tell that first; hear it, O good people.
The great accomplished master Nizami Ganjavi —
when he composed the book, reflecting in his mind —
at that time the king was fierce in his majesty.
Immovable as a mountain — Alauddin of lofty rule.
His glories and virtues — within the book,
Nizami, the king of poets, told of them at length.

column1: প্রয়োজন নাহি মোর সে সব কথনে।
column3: I have no need to recount all of that.
My mind is freed by the virtues of the king.
Description of Rosang and the King
(in long metre)

column1: শ্রীমন্ত রোসাঙ্গ স্থল নাহি তাহে বলাবল
column3: Prosperous Rosang — there is no telling the strength of that land,
it is encircled and studded with gold and gems.

column1: বৈসে সাধু সৎলোক সদত আনন্দ ভোগ
column3: Good people and honest folk dwell there, ever enjoying happiness;
crops and fish always abound there.
There reigns an incomparable king, by name Sri Chandra Sudharma,
the destroyer of the wicked and the refuge of the suffering.

column1: পুত্রবৎ প্রজা পাল বিপক্ষ জনের কাল
column3: He tends his subjects like a father his sons, and is death to those who stand against him,
a righteous and great overlord.

column1: নয়ান ধবল ভানু অতুল নিন্দিত তনু
column3: His eyes outshine the bright sun; his form is unrivalled and puts all else to shame;
with a sidelong glance he bewitches the noblewomen.

column1: অঙ্গ রঙ্গ ভঙ্গযুলে আকুল রমণী কুলে
column3: With the grace and play of his limbs, women of all families become distracted;
his speech is sweeter than nectar.
His palace is ringed with gold; it is filled and brimming with gems and jewels;
the seat is a divine throne of gold.

column1: হয় হস্তি নাই লেখা পয়দল হীন সংখ্যা
column3: Horses and elephants are beyond counting; the foot-soldiers are without number;
they move blocking even the path of the wind.
The king, an avatar of the lion, defeats elephants in an instant;
he goes with his fourfold army in attendance.

column1: মধ্যেম কর্দম পায় অগ্রগামী জলে যায়
column3: Those in the middle wade through mud; those in the vanguard march through water;
those in the rear are grey with dust.

column1: সৈন্যের পদের রেণু ঝাঁপয় গগণ ভানু
column3: The dust of the soldiers' feet leaps up and covers the sun in the sky;
war-parasols of many colours move forward.

column1: পবন সঘন লোলে উপরে চামর দোলে
column3: The breeze stirs densely; above, the fly-whisks sway;
it is as though one sees waves of lightning.

column1: শ্বেত রক্ত হেমমময় নানা বর্ণ ছত্রচয়
column3: Parasols of white, red, and gold, of many colours,
are studded with gems, pearls, and jewels.

column1: সমুখ অরুণ শশি নক্ষত্র সহিতে আসি
column3: Like the morning sun advancing with the moon and stars together,
the king moves forward on his march.

column1: বহিমিক অধিপতি নানা বর্ণ নানা ভাতি
column3: The lords of the outer regions, of many kinds and many appearances,
in this manner set their fly-whisks dancing.

column1: সমুদ্র জিনিয়া পাতি পবন জিনিয়া গতি
column3: He spreads wider than the ocean; his speed surpasses the wind;
at his roar the ranks of enemies tremble.

column1: মনেতে ভাবিয়া ডর নৃপকুলে দেয় কর
column3: Thinking of him in their hearts with fear, kings pay him tribute;
his domain stretches beyond seas and mountains.

column1: দিলীস্বর বংশ আসি যাহার স্মরণে পশি
column3: The lineage of the lord of Delhi comes into memory when one calls him to mind;
whose glory equals his?

column1: যুবকালে ব্রত ধর্ম শাস্ত্র নীতি সত্য কর্ম
column3: In youth he took to vows, righteousness, scripture, right conduct, and truthful deeds;
in charity and wisdom he is without limit.

column1: অপার মহিষ্মা সিন্ধু ক্ষুদ্র বুদ্ধি এক বিন্দু
column3: His glory is a boundless ocean; a feeble intellect is but a single drop of it;
what power do I have to speak of it?

column1: যত কাল চন্দ্র সূর কীর্তি মহি ভরপুর
column3: As long as the moon and the sun endure, may his fame fill the earth;
may his life and glory ever increase.

column1: হৈয়দ মহামদ গুণি মহত্ত আরতি শুনি
column3: Hearing the greatness and longing of the virtuous Sayyid Muhammad,
the lowly poet Alaol sings.
Description of the One Who Commanded the Writing of This Book
(in jamak metre — Kamod raga)
Such a great lord of kings, of unbroken wealth —
his vow of renunciation is Sayyid Muhammad, of void-mind.

column1: অঙ্গ দুর্বাদল শ্যাম মুখ পূর্ণ শশি।
column3: His body is dark as a cluster of durba grass, his face the full moon;
his speech is mingled with nectar, his lips carry a gentle smile.

column1: মদন কোদণ্ড ভুরু আঁখি পদ্ম নীল।
column3: His brows are Kama's bow, his eyes blue as the lotus;
by his sidelong glance noblewomen are bewitched and abandon virtue.
His frame is well-formed, his conduct graceful and beautiful;
the colour, beauty, and fragrance of his body are most pure.
He is proficient in many scriptures, learned and wise;
accomplished in Arabic, Persian, and also Hindavi Magadhi.

column1: সদত অতিথী ভক্ত গুনি মন জ্ঞাতা।
column3: Ever a devotee of guests, a knower of the minds of the virtuous;

column1: বিত্তি জিনী চিত্ত গেল সত্যমন্ত দাতা॥
column3: a truthful giver whose heart has transcended wealth.
What a benefactor of creation he is in the gift of speech —

column1: লোক মন তুষ্ট করে মিষ্ট সম্ভাষণে॥
column3: he satisfies the hearts of people with his sweet address.
Forgiving in heart, blameless in duty and deed;
for that reason his glory ever grows.

column1: ইষ্ট মিত্র বন্ধু আদি সবান পালক।
column3: He is the sustainer of all — cherished ones, friends, and companions alike;
the rising moon of his lineage, the ornament of his line.
His heart is devoted to the divine, to the teacher, and to the learned;

column1: ধর্ম কর্ম দানে মানে দেশ হরিষত॥
column3: in duty, deed, giving, and honour, the land rejoices.

column1: আর কি মহিমা আমি কহিব তাহান।
column3: What further glory shall I speak of his?

column1: মোহন সংক্ষিপ্ত জ্ঞাতা ভাব রস জান॥
column3: He is captivating, brief, a knower, understanding the essence and the rasa.

column1: নবি কুল হৈয়দ জাতি জাতির প্রধান।
column3: Of the Prophet's lineage, a Sayyid by birth, foremost among peoples;

column1: নিশি দিশী রাগ রঙে বিষাদ থাকেন॥
column3: night and day he abides in passion and colour and melancholy.
Ever in his court are the learned and the virtuous;

column1: তত্ত রস কথা কহি থাকেন সদায়॥
column3: they ever speak of matters of essence and rasa.

column1: নানা প্রস্তাব নানা গ্রন্থ অতি সুকথন।
column3: Many topics, many books, spoken of most beautifully;

column1: আনন্দে শুনেন সেবে হই এক মন।
column3: he listens with joy, and all become of one mind in that service.
I too remain in his court without cease;

column1: অন্ন বস্ত্র দানে আমা পোষেন্তু সদত॥
column3: with gifts of food and clothing he ever nourishes me.

column1: মোর মন রস তান প্রেম রাগ রায়।
column3: My heart delights in his love, passion, and melody;
with particular affection and grace he spoke to me.
Remaining among his courtiers, becoming one of the court,

column1: শাস্ত্র নীতি রস কথা প্রসঙ্গ কহিয়া।
column3: and speaking of the matters of scripture, counsel, and rasa —
one night, in the assembly of the learned, that great one
was seated in his own dwelling, in the savour of conversation.

column1: আমা প্রতি হৈলা আজ্ঞা হরিষত মনে।
column3: To me came the command, with a joyful heart,
to tell a certain excellent story.
The tale of the Sapta Paykar is most charming —
I disclosed what was in my heart before him.

column1: যেন মতে নৃপ এক পির বস্ত্র শ্যাম।
column3: How a certain king, wearing the dark garb of a dervish,
wept without rest, night and day, unceasingly —

column1: পশ্চাত কহিমু দেখি না কহিনু এথা।
column3: I shall tell it later; I did not tell it here;

column1: মহা উল্লাসিত হেল শুনি সেই কথা॥
column3: hearing that account, he became greatly elated.
Then, even as he smiled, he gave me the command:

column1: যত্ন করি এই কথা পয়ারে রিচিতে॥
column3: to compose this tale with care in the payar metre.
In Persian and Arabic the metre of this is called etaraj —
Nizami's composition is especially an ocean of words.

column1: এই গ্রন্থ মাঝে আর যত ইতিহাস।
column3: All the other narratives that are in this book —
set them forth in the manner of payar verse.
He is in particular a great man, a nourisher —
the scriptures call the giver of food equal to a father.

column1: তান আজ্ঞা লংঘিতে না পারি কদাচিত।
column3: I cannot ever transgress his command.
Even though my mind is worn out with age and burdened with anxiety.
Even if I am unfit for composing a book,

column1: তান ভাগ্য লক্ষ্যে হয় সমুদ্রে সঁকার॥
column3: by the sign of his fortune one plunges into the ocean.

column1: যেন চন্দ্র ধরিতে বালক হস্ত তোলে।
column3: As a child raises his hand to grasp the moon,
my only trust is at the feet of my master.
The uneven becomes even at the command of the great one;
darkness becomes light by the grace of the master.

column1: এত ভাবি সাহস করিলুং মতি হীনে।
column3: Thinking thus, I, the feeble-minded one, took courage,
and with devotion and obeisance I bow at the master's feet.

column1: নিজ গুণে শোধিও বিচারি পাইলে দোষ।
column3: By your own virtue, weigh and correct whatever fault you find.
Do not be displeased without due consideration.

column1: গ্রন্থের গ্রাহক কর্ম ছোট শক্তি নয়।
column3: The task of the discerning reader of a book is no small ability;
only the learned understand that essence.

column1: ভাব রস শব্দ অষ্টগণ লঘু গুরু।
column3: Sentiment, rasa, word, the eight qualities, the light and the weighty —

column1: ছন্দ উক্তি শুদ্ধ হলে গাঁথনি সুচারু॥
column3: when metre and expression are pure, the composition becomes excellent.
The great poet writes having weighed all these things;

column1: নেহ নিজ মনোগত সেবে কেহ ভাবি॥
column3: it is not merely what one privately fancies in one's own mind.

column1: কবি বাক্য রশ দহ অঙ্গ যুবা স্তন।
column3: The poet's words are like the breast of a young woman in their ten qualities of beauty —

column1: আন ভাতি যে মোহয় রশিকের মন॥
column3: they enchant the connoisseur's mind with a splendour unlike any other.

column1: দাতা দান হেন্তে বাক্য হয় রস যুক্তা।
column3: Just as the giver's gift, so words become filled with savour;
by offering the water of the ears, the oyster shell yields up the pearl.

column1: যে বলে বলুক আমি দৈবে হীন মতি।
column3: Let whoever will say what they will — by fate I am of feeble intellect.
The Lord of the three worlds alone is without fault.

column1: সমুদ্রেত ডুব দিলে করি বহ যত্ন॥
column3: If one dives into the ocean with great effort,

column1: কেহ বট পায় কেহ বহুমূল্য রত্ন
column3: some find a fig, some find a precious jewel —
even so,
there are many buyers for the jewel,

column1: সদত কার্যেত লাগে বট অল্প মূল॥
column3: yet the fig of modest value is ever of use in daily need.
The sweet, fine fruit of the garden —
the lemon is always of use for small everyday needs.
Those who went before have plundered all the treasure of merit;

column1: বহ যত্রে অল্প পায় পৃষ্টগামী জন॥
column3: despite much effort, those who come after obtain but little.
Thinking thus, let everyone forgive me;

column1: যে বলে বলয় যত্রি যন্ত্রে সেই বলে॥
column3: whoever will say what they will — the instrument speaks through the one who plays it.
Whatever is new and whatever is old —
the jewel of speech has become the best of all things.
Of all that has arisen in the intimate circle of the people of the world,
none has been born equal to speech as an offspring.

column1: না কহিলে কথন সকল থাকে ধন্দ।
column3: If speech is not uttered, all things remain in confusion;

column1: প্রকাশ করিলে সেবে বুঝে ভাল মন্দ॥
column3: when it is made manifest, then the mind understands good and bad.
The unseen, faultless word is like the living soul itself;
the jewel from the treasure-house of the book is utterly incomparable.
Whatever depths are unheard, speech brings them to light.
All of history is revealed through it.
Consider how much the Creator has fashioned as weighty.

column1: কেবল বচন বিনে কিবা আছে আর॥
column3: Yet without speech alone, what else is there?
All the secrets of the past that remain in memory —

column1: বুঝহ বচন বিনে আর কেহ নয়॥
column3: understand that without speech, none other can grasp them.

column1: যদি অন্য রত্ন হৈত বচন সমতুল।
column3: If any other jewel were equal to speech —

column1: বাক্যজালে শুক সারি পক্ষি বহ মূল॥
column3: in the net of words, parrots and mynas carry great worth.

column1: বুদ্ধি বাক্য হেন্তে বহ সৃজিল ঈশ্বরে।
column3: God created intellect and speech in such abundance.
Who then is capable of telling of His greatness?
Through the very faculty of intellect, one's own knowledge becomes inferior.

column1: এথা ওথা নিত্য নিত্য বাড়য় সম্মান॥
column3: Here and there, honour ever grows and grows.
Know your own inner self according to your knowledge.
Yet some people still fall into a measure of error.
If one considers oneself even slightly superior, it is self-disgrace.

column1: জ্ঞানবন্ত মনে সেই নেহ বস্তু জ্ঞান॥
column3: In the mind of the wise, that is no proper knowledge.
His foolishness does not diminish day by day.
Through intellect, he does not regard himself as experienced.
Yet all people, by the marks of the age of Kali —

column1: মূড় বুদ্ধি অমূল নবুজে কোন জনে॥
column3: with dull minds, no one understands the worthless from the valuable.
In the heart of the clever, nothing is ever hidden.
Who has greater store, and who is barren and low.

column1: রিতে ধনবন্ত জেন সকলেরে ভাষে ধনি।
column3: In the same way, the wealthy call everyone else wealthy.
The virtueless regards the virtuous as if they too were virtueless.
Although everyone seeks their own good —
the mind of the virtuous desires the good of others.

column1: কেবল আপন হিত চিন্তে যেই জন।
column3: The person who thinks only of his own benefit —

column1: পর অপকার কথা আছে তার মন॥
column3: in his mind dwells the thought of harming others.

column1: যার চিত্ত মন্দ ভাব মন্দ ফল পায়।
column3: Whoever has an evil disposition reaps an evil result.
The good person passes both here and there in well-being.
If a thorn pierces, endure it steadily and with patience.

column1: হীন স্থানে কহিলে না লৈব দুঃখ বাটা॥
column3: If you speak of it in a low place, you will receive no share of sympathy.

column1: দুঃখ পাই কান্দিলে হাসয় খল জন।
column3: If you weep in grief, the wicked laugh.
The base folk do not understand the ordination of fate.

column1: তার পাশে দুঃখ না কহিও কদাচিত।
column3: Never at any time speak of your grief to such a person.
Seeing a person overwhelmed with worry, he is filled with joy.

column1: ক্ষুধাতুর জন আগে না খাইও রুটি।
column3: Do not eat bread in front of a hunger-stricken person.

column1: যদি খাও সকলের দিবা কিছু বাঁটি॥
column3: If you do eat, give some share to everyone there.
If you weigh gold in front of a penniless man —

column1: দেখি অঙ্গ মোড়ায় ধনের সর্প প্রায়॥
column3: seeing it, he writhes in his body like the serpent of wealth.

column1: যদি বা বসন্ত বায়ু সুশীতল লাগে।
column3: And if the cool spring breeze feels pleasant —

column1: উষ্ণমন্ত দীপ ন জালিবা তার আগে॥
column3: do not light a hot lamp before one who suffers from heat.

column1: বুদ্ধি পাছে দামে সে উজ্জুল কলেবর।
column3: Intellect afterwards endows that radiant body with value.

column1: তৃণ ভক্ষ হেতু প্রভু ন সৃজিছে নর॥
column3: The Lord did not create man merely to graze on grass.

column1: সে মনুষ্য ধিক জেন শীসের মাহাত্ত্য।
column3: Shame on that man who is like the greatness of a blade of grass —

column1: যেবে দৃষ্টি তৃণ পরে গর্দভের মত॥
column3: whose gaze falls on grass in the manner of a donkey.

column1: সে সেই মনুষ্য যেই করে পর হিত।
column3: He alone is truly a man who does good for others.
Or who satisfies the heart of the afflicted through giving.

column1: রুক্ষ দানে যত পুষ্প চন্দন সুগন্ধ।
column3: Like flowers and fragrant sandalwood given as a harsh gift.

column1: নিস্ফল মনুষ্য জন্ম হৈলে মতি মন্দ॥
column3: Human birth becomes fruitless when the mind turns base.
Thus I have heard from the mouth of great scholars.

column1: দিব্য ভাবে শুতিলে স্বপন ভাল দেখে॥
column3: One who sleeps in a divine state sees good dreams.

column1: জন্ম কাল তাহার মরণ অতি ভাল।
column3: Good is the birth and good is the death of such a one.
Whose character remains base right up to the hour of death.
One who is born with a thoroughly pure character —
Shame on life and death; only fame endures.
The fruit is the span of life; death is in every way.

column1: যাহার মরণে লাগে লোক মনে ব্যথা॥
column3: He at whose death the hearts of people feel pain.

column1: কঠিনতা গর্ব তেজে যদি হয় ভাল
column3: If one abandons harshness and pride and becomes good —
In time of calamity, you will find death upon your head.
The great always regard such a one as a great man.
The body fashioned from clay is near in nature to clay.
The clay is dark and impure, yet the memory shines bright.
Among flowers, the rose flower keeps company with thorns.

column1: কে বুঝিতে পারয় ঈশ্বর সুষ্কনু লীলা॥
column3: Who can comprehend the subtle sport of the Lord?

column1: সর্প হেন্তে উপজয় রিপু জীর্ণ শীলা।
column3: From the snake arises the enemy of decayed stone.

column1: বিষে বিষ জন্য মণি আছে সর্প সঙ্গে॥
column3: In the poison, on account of poison, a jewel exists in the company of the snake.

column1: কেবল কুমতি বিনা নাহি খল অঙ্গে।
column3: Save for ill-will alone, there is nothing in the body of the wicked.

column1: জ্ঞানবন্ত কর্ম যদি করিতে না পারে॥
column3: If one possessed of wisdom is unable to act —

column1: নানা মতে কহি খল জলি জলি মরে।
column3: I say in many ways: the wicked burn and burn and die.
In the fire of their own mind they themselves become confused.

column1: খল ত জানায় উত্তমেরে বলি মন্দ।
column3: The wicked indeed make known the noble by calling them bad.

column1: বুদ্ধি স্থির করিয়া ভাবিয়া যদি চায়॥
column3: If one settles the mind, reflects, and then looks —

column1: ন জানে যাহার মর্ম শিখিতে জুয়ায়।
column3: One who does not know its inner meaning is fit to learn.

column1: দীপ জীব প্রভা হীন বুদ্ধি তুল্য বিনু॥
column3: A lamp deprived of life and light is comparable to one without intellect.

column1: বুদ্ধি সঙ্গে জীবন সতত জীব মনু।
column3: With intellect, life is always alive in the human mind.

column1: বুদ্ধি সে জীবন তার জীব সত্য বেন॥
column3: Intellect is truly the life of that life, the very truth of living.
One who serves receives the gift of death through the grace of the Lord.

column1: আর নানা ভাঁতি কথা বহুবিধ নিত॥
column3: And there are many other kinds of discourse, manifold and constant.
The nature of creation, the great, and the wicked.
The great Nizami has spoken of this in the earlier part.

column1: কহিতে সে সব কথা নাহি পারি আমি।
column3: I am not able to tell all those things.
The scholar who is a treasury of virtues understands more on his own.

column1: সমুখে পুস্তক কথা আছে মহা ভার।
column3: Ahead the matter of the book weighs very heavy.
For that reason I abandon lengthy speech.

column1: পুস্তকের সুর কেবা শুন ধীর জন॥
column3: Now hear, O patient souls, the melody of the book.
The Start of the Story
(in long metre)

column1: নরমান নামে রাজা বহ লোকে করে পুজা
column3: A king by the name of Noman, worshipped by many people —
Lord of Arab and Ajam.
His incomparable son, Bahram-Gor by name —
Was born as offspring in Iraq.

column1: পুত্র মুখ দেখি রাজা করিয়া ঈশ্বর পূজা
column3: Beholding the face of his son, the king worshipped the Lord,
Thinking in his heart of the hope of his son's welfare.
Forsaking his birthplace and placing him with worthy persons,
He gave him to dwell in such a land.

column1: কর্মি এক অনুপাম ছমনা তাহার নাম
column3: A matchless craftsman — Samana was his name,
the king bestowed him as a companion to his son.

column1: এক গৃহে সপ্ত টঙ্গি হেম রত্নে সপ্ত রঙি
column3: In one dwelling, seven towers, seven-coloured with gold and gems,
he had the house built and given to his son.

column1: হয় হস্তি আরোহণ অস্ত্রে শাস্ত্রে বিদ্যাগুণ
column3: Having become skilled in horse and elephant riding, in arms and scriptures, in learning and virtues,
the prince was praised by the king, having attained full mastery.

column1: মৃগয়া করয় নিত মনে না করয় ভিত
column3: He hunted every day, feeling no fear in his heart,
he killed lions and serpents wondrously.

column1: মহা ধনুর্ধর হইয়া রাজ কার্য তেয়াগিয়া
column3: Being a great bowman, he abandoned the affairs of the kingdom,
and spent his time joyfully in dance and song.

column1: তাহা শুনি রিপুগণ সাজি আইল কত জন
column3: Hearing this, many enemies armed themselves and came,
yet by the power of his wit he conquered them with ease.

column1: নরমান পিতা তার মরি গেল যম দ্বার
column3: Numan, his father, died and went through the gate of Yama,
and the ministers of Yemen became the masters there.

column1: শুনিয়া সে সব কথা সৈসন্য পৌঁছিল তথা
column3: Hearing all of this, he arrived there with his army,
and drew up his forces on two sides.

column1: নিয়ম করিলা সার দুই ব্যাঘ্র আনিবার
column3: The principal decree was made to bring two tigers,
and to place the crown in between them.

column1: দীপি যুগ মধ্য হেন্তে যে পারয় তাজ নিতে
column3: Whoever could retrieve the crown from between the pair of leopards,
to him the kingdom would be subject.

column1: করিয়া নিয়ম কর্ম বুঝিয়া কর্মের মর্ম
column3: Having established this rule and understanding the import of the deed,
the two kings came to that place.

column1: এরাকের পতি আসে না আইল ব্যাঘ্র পাশে
column3: The lord of Yemen came but could not approach the tigers,
and Bahram took the crown and went away.

column1: দেখিয়া বাহরাম শক্তি করিয়া বিবিধ ভক্তি
column3: Beholding Bahram's power, and showing manifold reverence,
he quickly came and fell at his feet in devotion.

column1: পিতৃ রাজ্য ধন পাইয়া সর্বলোকে অধিবাসিয়া
column3: Having obtained his father's kingdom and wealth, and settled all the people,
he came to his land with a joyful heart.

column1: পদে কণে মীগ হামি অভ্যাস বচন শুনি
column3: Hearing the words of customary address spoken humbly at his feet,
the command was given swiftly according to law.

column1: স্কন্ধে করি রথ এক টঙ্গি পরে তুলিলেক
column3: Carrying a chariot on his shoulders, he lifted it up onto a tower,
and seeing this, all became joyful once more.

column1: ভোষ সপ্ত রাজ জিনি সপ্ত রাজকন্যা আনি
column3: Conquering seven kings in battle, he brought the seven princesses,
and gave them dwelling in seven houses.

column1: আনন্দ উৎসবে রায় যে দিনে যে গৃহে যায়
column3: The king went in festive joy — whichever day, to whichever house he went,
thereafter he wore the colour of that house.

column1: নৃত্য গীতে অবশেষে গোয়াইলা কেলি রেসে
column3: After dance and song, he passed time in the rasa of sport,
it being Bahram's hour of repose.

column1: কেহ রাজ কন্যা প্রতি শুনেই গুণবতী
column3: To each princess, who was heard to be virtuous,
he said: tell one excellent tale.

column1: এই মতে সপ্ত রাতি সপ্ত বিভিন্ন কলাবতী
column3: In this manner, over seven nights, seven different women of art
told seven fine tales.

column1: এই পুস্তকের সূত্র শুন গুণি সাধু পুত্র
column3: This is the thread of this book — listen, virtuous and noble son,
it is a wave of nectar on the ocean of rasa.

column1: হৈয়দ মহামদ গুণি ধর্মশীল দানে মানি
column3: Sayyid Muhammad, virtuous, righteous in conduct, honoured for generosity,
having received his venerable command,

column1: কেহ আলাওল হীন সুর শশি যত দিন
column3: may Alaol, this humble one, as long as the sun and moon endure,
may his life, fame, and auspiciousness increase.
